title: Gutter Calculator
shortDescription: Estimate gutter runs, hangers, and downspouts for a specific roof layout.
metaDescription: Estimate gutter length, hangers, and downspouts from eave length, roof edges, spacing, and waste allowance.
intro: Enter your eave length, roof edge count, hanger spacing, downspout spacing, and waste allowance to estimate the gutter materials you need.
overview:
This gutter calculator gives you a practical material estimate for a roof drainage setup. It converts the roof edge length you enter into a total gutter run, then estimates the number of hangers and downspouts based on the spacing you choose.

Use it when you are planning a new installation, replacing damaged sections, or comparing material quantities before placing an order. The waste allowance helps account for cuts, overlaps, end pieces, and on-site corrections, so the estimate is closer to what you will actually need.
howItWorks: Measure the eave or roof edge length in meters.
Enter how many edges will receive gutters.
Set the planned distance between downspouts in meters.
Add a waste allowance for cuts and joints.
Review the calculated gutter length, hangers, and downspouts.

useCases.title: New roof drainage layout
useCases.description: Work out the materials for a fresh gutter system on a house, garage, or annex before ordering parts.

useCases.title: Replacement after storm damage
useCases.description: Estimate how many sections, hangers, and downspouts are needed when only part of the system is being renewed.

useCases.title: Multi-side roof planning
useCases.description: Compare material needs for different roof edges when each side has a different length or outlet layout.

useCases.title: Material order check
useCases.description: Confirm whether a quoted parts list matches the roof dimensions and spacing you measured on site.

examples.title: Two-sided house roof
examples.input: Eave length: 12 m, roof edges: 2, hanger spacing: 60 cm, downspout spacing: 10 m, waste: 10%
examples.output: Estimated total gutter run: 26.4 m. Hangers: 44. Downspouts: 3.
examples.description: A typical detached house layout with one outlet planned per roof side and a small allowance for cuts.

examples.title: Single garage roof
examples.input: Eave length: 8.5 m, roof edges: 1, hanger spacing: 50 cm, downspout spacing: 7 m, waste: 5%
examples.output: Estimated total gutter run: 8.9 m. Hangers: 18. Downspouts: 2.
examples.description: Useful for a compact roof where even a short run still needs a safe hanger count.

examples.title: Long workshop building
examples.input: Eave length: 24 m, roof edges: 2, hanger spacing: 70 cm, downspout spacing: 12 m, waste: 8%
examples.output: Estimated total gutter run: 51.8 m. Hangers: 74. Downspouts: 5.
examples.description: Helps when a long building needs several outlets to avoid overloading one section.

faq.question: Does this calculator include corners and end caps automatically?
faq.answer: No. Corners, end caps, connectors, and outlets are usually counted separately. Use the gutter length result as the base run, then add those fittings from your roof layout.

faq.question: Why can the hanger count be higher than I expected?
faq.answer: The calculator uses the spacing you enter across the full gutter length, so shorter spacing produces more hangers. Many installations also need extra hangers near corners, outlets, and joins.

faq.question: How should I treat a roof with different edge lengths?
faq.answer: Calculate each edge separately and add the results. Do not use one average length if the sides have very different runs or different drain points.

faq.question: What does the waste allowance cover in practice?
faq.answer: It covers cut losses, overlaps, fitting adjustments, and small mistakes made during installation. It is not the same as the number of connectors or special fittings you may still need to buy.

faq.question: Can I use the downspout result as the exact number of outlets?
faq.answer: No. The downspout figure is an estimate based on spacing, not a design for every drainage point. Final outlet placement depends on roof pitch, runoff volume, and where water can be safely discharged.

seo.title: Gutter Calculator for Roof Drainage
seo.description: Estimate gutter length, hangers, and downspouts from eave length, roof edges, spacing, and waste allowance.
